criteria_verification.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/retry.py exporting class RetryCoordinator with async method retry_with_context(*, previous_outcome, capability, original_parameters, additional_context). Verified by tests TestRetryCoordinatorSurface.test_module_defines_retry_coordinator_class and test_retry_with_context_is_async (both passing).
criteria_verification.notes: Created src/forge/dispatch/retry.py exporting class RetryCoordinator with async method retry_with_context(*, previous_outcome, capability, original_parameters, additional_context). Verified by tests TestRetryCoordinatorSurface.test_module_defines_retry_coordinator_class and test_retry_with_context_is_async (both passing).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: No fixed max-retry count at this layer. The retry coordinator does
criteria_verification.evidence: Module docstring in src/forge/dispatch/retry.py opens with 'This module is intentionally policy-free. There is no fixed max-retry count at this layer (ASSUM-005)' and includes a 'Why no max-retry counter here' section explaining the reasoning loop owns retry policy. Implementation has no counter or refusal logic — every call forwards directly to orchestrator.dispatch(). Verified by TestNoFixedMaxRetryDocumented.test_module_docstring_documents_no_max_retry (passing) which asserts the docstring mentions both 'max-retry' and 'reasoning'.
criteria_verification.notes: Module docstring in src/forge/dispatch/retry.py opens with 'This module is intentionally policy-free. There is no fixed max-retry count at this layer (ASSUM-005)' and includes a 'Why no max-retry counter here' section explaining the reasoning loop owns retry policy. Implementation has no counter or refusal logic — every call forwards directly to orchestrator.dispatch(). Verified by TestNoFixedMaxRetryDocumented.test_module_docstring_documents_no_max_retry (passing) which asserts the docstring mentions both 'max-retry' and 'reasoning'.

criteria_verification.evidence: Implementation computes next_attempt_no = previous_outcome.attempt_no + 1 and passes retry_of=previous_outcome.resolution_id to orchestrator.dispatch(); the orchestrator then stamps retry_of onto the persisted CapabilityResolution copy. Verified by TestRetryWithAdditionalContext: test_retry_outcome_attempt_no_is_previous_plus_one (passes assertion retry.attempt_no == 2) and test_retry_persists_retry_of_link_to_previous_resolution (passes assertion exactly one persisted row carries retry_of=first.resolution_id).
criteria_verification.notes: Implementation computes next_attempt_no = previous_outcome.attempt_no + 1 and passes retry_of=previous_outcome.resolution_id to orchestrator.dispatch(); the orchestrator then stamps retry_of onto the persisted CapabilityResolution copy. Verified by TestRetryWithAdditionalContext: test_retry_outcome_attempt_no_is_previous_plus_one (passes assertion retry.attempt_no == 2) and test_retry_persists_retry_of_link_to_previous_resolution (passes assertion exactly one persisted row carries retry_of=first.resolution_id).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Test (fresh correlation): the new dispatch attempt's correlation
criteria_verification.evidence: RetryCoordinator does NOT pass through any correlation key — the orchestrator's CorrelationRegistry.fresh_correlation_key() issues a new 32-hex token per dispatch. Verified by TestFreshCorrelationKey.test_retry_uses_fresh_correlation_key_not_previous_one (passing): asserts publisher.published[0].attempt.correlation_key != publisher.published[1].attempt.correlation_key, that both match [0-9a-f]{32}, and that both keys appear exactly once in transport.subscribed_keys (the registry's binding-map projection).
criteria_verification.notes: RetryCoordinator does NOT pass through any correlation key — the orchestrator's CorrelationRegistry.fresh_correlation_key() issues a new 32-hex token per dispatch. Verified by TestFreshCorrelationKey.test_retry_uses_fresh_correlation_key_not_previous_one (passing): asserts publisher.published[0].attempt.correlation_key != publisher.published[1].attempt.correlation_key, that both match [0-9a-f]{32}, and that both keys appear exactly once in transport.subscribed_keys (the registry's binding-map projection).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Test (sibling not overwrite): after retry, BOTH resolution records
criteria_verification.evidence: Verified by TestSiblingResolutionRecord.test_retry_creates_sibling_record_and_preserves_original (passing): asserts len(rows_after) == len(rows_before) + 1, asserts the original row equals its pre-retry snapshot via Pydantic structural equality, and asserts original.retry_of remains None. Also covered by the verbatim seam test TestSeamCapabilityResolutionSiblingContract.test_retry_creates_sibling_record_not_overwrite.
criteria_verification.notes: Verified by TestSiblingResolutionRecord.test_retry_creates_sibling_record_and_preserves_original (passing): asserts len(rows_after) == len(rows_before) + 1, asserts the original row equals its pre-retry snapshot via Pydantic structural equality, and asserts original.retry_of remains None. Also covered by the verbatim seam test TestSeamCapabilityResolutionSiblingContract.test_retry_creates_sibling_record_not_overwrite.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Test (additional context propagation): parameters list passed to
criteria_verification.evidence: Implementation uses list concatenation (original_parameters + additional_context), explicitly NOT in-place extend. Verified by TestAdditionalContextPropagation: test_orchestrator_receives_original_then_additional_in_order (asserts published params equal [("goal","ship"), ("repo","forge"), ("hint","check tests"), ("severity","high")] in exact order) and test_retry_does_not_mutate_caller_provided_lists (asserts id() and len() of caller-provided lists are unchanged after the call).

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 360 chars provided.
